title: Watchers v2: events route through the agent for analysis, not dumb notifications
current_state: Watchers (file/url/price/schedule/keyword) fire raw Telegram notifications ('file changed'). Pi has the full toolset to ANALYZE what changed and say why it matters, but watchers bypass the agent entirely.
target_state: A watcher event optionally carries analyze=True: the event text becomes a turn through process_input in a dedicated 'watchers' conversation (T-186 store, never bleeding into Ash's active chats), normie-tier by default (free), and the ANALYZED result goes to Telegram ('pi_agent.py changed: the tool loop gained parallel dispatch — tests still green'). Raw mode stays for cheap pings.
migration_plan: 1. WatcherManager gains an agent handle (it already gets telegram_send_fn; same injection pattern).
2. analyze=True events: switch-restore-persist around process_input on conversation 'watchers' (reuse the T-187/T-188 helper — one pattern, three callers).
3. Cost guard: analyzed events rate-limited (max N/hour) + normie tier so a hot file can't burn budget.
4. Tests: analyzed event lands in the watchers conversation not the active one; rate limit enforced; raw mode unchanged.
risk_notes: The conversation-isolation invariant (T-142) is the safety here — an autonomous turn must NEVER splice into Ash's active thread. Depends on T-201 so the analysis turn has read-tools on the free tier.
